warnings.message: Some PDF pages have no strong text-window match in the EPUB
Some dense PDF word windows are missing from the EPUB and may indicate sliced sentence interiors
Some first body lines near the top of PDF pages are not found in the EPUB
Some last body lines near the bottom of PDF pages are not found in the EPUB
Some adjacent EPUB paragraphs look like possible faulty line or page breaks
Some list or roman markers appear embedded in prose instead of starting their own paragraph
Some roman numeral headings appear in body paragraphs instead of centered heading elements
Some chapter headings are long enough to suggest swallowed body text
Repeated word windows may indicate ghost-layer duplication

THE SPIRIT A SEAL, AND HOW.

roman_heading_candidates.text: II. That there are external arguments of the divine original of the Scripture, which are effectual motives to persuade us to give an unfeigned assent thereunto:
III. That yet, moreover, God requires of us that we believe them to be his word with faith divine, supernatural, and infallible:
IV. Evidence the grounds and reasons whereon we do so believe, and ought so to do.

II. This being the substance of what is declared and pleaded for in the preceding treatise, to prevent the obloquy of some and confirm the judgment of others, I shall add the suffrage of ancient and modern writers given
I. The Holy, Spirit is represented as the EFFICIENT CAUSE, and an inquiry follows: —

II. That as to the right understanding of the mind of God in the Scripture, or our coming unto the riches of the full assurance of understanding in the acknowledgment of the mystery of God, we do not, nor need to depend

III. That in the mere exercise of our own natural reason and understanding, with the help of external means, we cannot attain that knowledge of the mind and wfil of God in the Scripture, of the sense and meaning of the H

V. That hereby alone is that full assurance of understanding in the knowledge of the mystery of God, his truth and grace, to be obtained, whereby any man may answer the mind and will of God, or comply with his own duty i

VII. That whereas the means of the right interpretation of the Scripture, and understanding of the mind of God therein, are of two sorts, — first, such as are prescribed unto us in a way of duty, as prayer, meditation on
II. The means designed for the improvement hereof, or our profitable use of it, are of three sorts: —
I. The evidence of its reality consists in a minute explanation of two passages in Scripture, Zechariah 12:10, and Galatians 4:6, 2, 3.
II. Its general nature is considered, — prayer having been defined to be a spiritual faculty of exercising Christian graces in the way of vocal requests and supplications to God,

I. In our inquiry after the first, or what are the gifts whereby men are fitted and enabled for the ministry, we wholly set aside the consideration of all those gracious qualifications of faith, love, zeal, compassion, c
II. It remains only that we inquire how men may come unto or attain a participation of these gifts, whether ministerial or more private. And unto this end we may observe, —
